root_text.an4.58:1.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.58:2.1: “Bhojanaṁ, gahapati, dadamāno ariyasāvako paṭiggāhakānaṁ cattāri ṭhānāni deti.
root_text.an4.58:2.3: Āyuṁ deti, vaṇṇaṁ deti, sukhaṁ deti, balaṁ deti.
root_text.an4.58:2.4: Āyuṁ kho pana datvā āyussa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an4.58:2.5: Vaṇṇaṁ datvā … sukhaṁ datvā … balaṁ datvā balassa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an4.58:2.6: Bhojanaṁ, gahapati, dadamāno ariyasāvako paṭiggāhakānaṁ imāni cattāri ṭhānāni detīti.

translation_text.an4.58:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an4.58:2.1: “Householder, when a noble disciple gives food, they give the recipients four things.
translation_text.an4.58:2.3: Long life, beauty, happiness, and strength.
translation_text.an4.58:2.4: Giving long life, they have long life as a god or human. …
translation_text.an4.58:2.6: When a noble disciple gives food, they give the recipients these four things.
translation_text.an4.58:3.1: Carefully giving food at the right time,
translation_text.an4.58:3.2: to those who are disciplined, <j>eating only what others give,
translation_text.an4.58:3.3: you provide them with four things:
translation_text.an4.58:3.4: long life, beauty, happiness, and strength.
translation_text.an4.58:4.1: A person who gives long life, beauty,

variant_text.an4.58:4.1: vaṇṇadāyī → so āyudāyī baladāyī (bj, pts1ed); āyudāyī baladāyī (sya-all, km)
variant_text.an4.58:4.2: sukhaṁ balaṁ dado → sukhaṁ vaṇṇaṁ dado (bj, sya-all, km); sukhavaṇṇadado (pts1ed); sukhabaladado (mr)
